title: Rapid Nuclei Isolation from Human Brain
author: Fatma Ayhan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.scpeavn
materials: Dounce homogenize tissue samples (smaller than 0.5 cm) or cell pellets in 2 ml of ice-cold Nuclei EZ lysis buffer (Sigma, #EZ PREP NUC-101). Dounce tissue 20-25 times with pestle A, followed by 20-25 times with pestle B while on ice.

steps.title: Prepare Nuclei Suspension Buffer (NSB)
steps.instruction: Prepare Nuclei Suspension Buffer (NSB): For drop-seq: 1x PBS, 0.01% BSA (ultra pure), 0.2U/ul RNAse inhibitor For 10X Genomics: 1X PBS, 1%BSA (ultra pure), 0.2U/ul RNAse inhibitor

steps.title: Move sample to a 15 ml...
steps.instruction: Move sample to a 15 ml conical tube, add 2 ml of ice-cold Nuclei EZ lysis buffer and incubate on ice for 5 min.

steps.title: Collect nuclei by centrifugation at 500...
steps.instruction: Collect nuclei by centrifugation at 500 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C. Discard supernatant and carefully resuspend nuclei in 4 ml of ice-cold Nuclei EZ lysis buffer. Incubate on ice for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Repeat the centrifugation at 500 x...
steps.instruction: Repeat the centrifugation at 500 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C. Resuspend isolated nuclei in 1 ml of NSB, and filter through a Flowmi Cell Strainer (Bel-Art, H13680-0040).

steps.title: Stain 10μl of nuclei suspension with DAPI (1:5000) or 0
steps.instruction: Stain 10μl of nuclei suspension with DAPI (1:5000) or 0.4% Trypan Blue to count.

steps.title: Adjust the volume with NSB to...
steps.instruction: Adjust the volume with NSB to 1000 nuclei/μl (for 10X Genomics) and 300 nuclei/μl (for drop-seq).
